data-cleanup-task.label: Data Cleanup Task
data-cleanup-task.summary: Use string functions and filtering to clean up data.

cleanup-intro.title: Why Data Cleanup Matters
cleanup-intro.bodyMarkdown: Real-world data often contains test records, inconsistent values, or outdated information. Cleaning up your data using SQL ensures that your analysis and reporting are accurate. In this topic, you'll practice identifying and removing test items, standardizing text, and filtering out unwanted records using the `inventory_items` table.

string-functions.title: Useful String Functions
string-functions.bodyMarkdown: SQL provides several string functions to help clean and standardize data. For example, you can use `UPPER()` or `LOWER()` to change text case, or `TRIM()` to remove extra spaces. Filtering out test or sample items can be done using `WHERE` clauses that check for keywords in the `name` or `notes` columns, or by using the `is_test` flag.

filtering-records.title: Filtering Unwanted Records
filtering-records.bodyMarkdown: To focus on real inventory, you may want to exclude items marked as test data (`is_test = 1`) or those with a status like 'draft' or 'inactive'. Combining multiple conditions in your `WHERE` clause helps you target exactly the records you want to keep or remove.

identify-test-items.title: Identifying Test Items
identify-test-items.prompt: Which column in the `inventory_items` table is best used to identify test or sample items for cleanup?
identify-test-items.hint: Look for a column that directly flags test data.

help.concept: The `is_test` column is an integer flag that indicates whether an item is a test/sample (1) or a real item (0).
help.hint_1: Check for a column that is a clear indicator, not just a text description.
help.hint_2: The best column is a numeric flag specifically for test items.

multi-cleanup-criteria.title: Choosing Cleanup Criteria
multi-cleanup-criteria.prompt: Which of the following criteria would help you filter out unwanted records when cleaning the `inventory_items` table? (Select all that apply.)
multi-cleanup-criteria.hint: Think about which options help you find test or non-final items.

help.concept: Filtering out test items and those not yet finalized (like 'draft' status) is common in data cleanup.
help.hint_1: Test items and drafts are usually not part of the final dataset.
help.hint_2: Look for flags or statuses that indicate the item is not ready for production.

drag-cleanup-steps.title: Order the Cleanup Steps
drag-cleanup-steps.prompt: Arrange these steps in the correct order for cleaning up the `inventory_items` table.
drag-cleanup-steps.hint: Start by identifying what to remove, then standardize, then review.

help.concept: A typical cleanup process first removes unwanted records, then standardizes data, and finally reviews the result.
help.hint_2: Reviewing is always the last step.

tokens.t2: Filter out items with status 'draft' or 'inactive'
tokens.t3: Standardize text case in the name column

fill-blank-string-func.title: Using String Functions
fill-blank-string-func.prompt: Which SQL function would you use to convert all product names to lowercase in the `inventory_items` table?
fill-blank-string-func.hint: Think about which function changes text to all small letters.

help.concept: The `the missing term()` function converts all characters in a string to lowercase.
help.hint_2: This function is often used to standardize text for comparison.

code-cleanup-query.title: Write a Cleanup Query
code-cleanup-query.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all columns from `inventory_items` where the item is NOT a test item (`is_test = 0`) AND has a status of 'active'.
code-cleanup-query.hint: Use a WHERE clause with both conditions joined by AND.

help.concept: Combining multiple conditions in a WHERE clause lets you filter for only the records you want.
help.hint_1: Check both the is_test flag and the status column.
help.hint_2: Both conditions must be true for a row to be included.
